evals.query: Create a 4K AWS PNG for a generic serverless web application: a browser loads static assets through CloudFront from S3 and calls API Gateway, which invokes Lambda to read and write DynamoDB.
expected_behavior: Defines a current-runtime perspective before rendering
Creates an explicit resource inventory, containment map, and relationship allowlist
Shows separate asset-delivery and API request paths without merging them ambiguously
Labels each resource by descriptive name and AWS service type
Does not fabricate VPC or subnet boundaries for the managed services
Inspects the generated image against every allowed and forbidden edge
Saves and verifies an exact 3840x2160 PNG

evals.query: Update our AWS architecture picture to show a current synchronous API Gateway to Lambda request path and a proposed asynchronous API Gateway to SQS to Lambda worker path. Put everything in one diagram.
expected_behavior: Rejects mixing current and future topology into an ambiguous master diagram
Creates separate current-runtime and proposed-asynchronous perspectives
Keeps the request path in each perspective limited to the services explicitly stated
Labels queue, worker, retry, and DLQ relationships explicitly in the scale-out view
Uses the documented SQS event source mapping relationship for Lambda workers
Uses the same visual system across both artifacts

evals.query: Generate an implementation-facing AWS architecture diagram from this repository's Terraform. Make it polished and use AWS icons.
expected_behavior: Reads Terraform and relevant documentation before deciding the topology
Uses actual logical resource roles and does not expose secrets or copy sensitive account identifiers
Separates runtime dependencies from deployment-time resources when both exist
Uses current official AWS documentation to verify unstable service behavior
Rejects any generated image whose arrows or boundaries disagree with Terraform
Keeps a reviewable contract or Mermaid source beside the PNG

evals.query: Draw a UML class diagram for these local Python data models.
expected_behavior: Does not invoke the AWS architecture diagram skill for a non-AWS UML class diagram
